home.title: Frequently Asked Questions
home.description: Quick answers about privacy, speed, and using PotatoTools.

items.question: Is PotatoTools free to use?
items.answer: Yes. Core tools are free to use and available instantly without signup.

items.question: Do I need an account to use these tools?
items.answer: No account is required. Open a tool, enter input, and get your result.

items.question: Does PotatoTools work on mobile?
items.answer: Yes. The interface is responsive and works on mobile, tablet, and desktop.

items.question: How accurate is this snow day prediction?
items.answer: It is an estimate based on your inputs and common closure patterns. Always verify with official district announcements.

items.question: Does it work for every school district?
items.answer: Policies vary by district. Use this as a planning signal, not a guaranteed official decision.

items.question: When should I check again?
items.answer: Check again closer to snowfall, especially 12 to 18 hours before the expected event.

text_to_handwriting_converter.title: Frequently Asked Questions
text_to_handwriting_converter.description: Quick answers about fonts, page styles, privacy, and exporting handwritten pages.

items.question: How does this text to handwriting converter work?
items.answer: You type or paste text, choose a page background, pick a handwriting font, and the tool renders your text onto a paper-style canvas in the browser.

items.question: Can I download the result as PNG and PDF?
items.answer: Yes. This version exports the current single-page preview as a PNG image or a PDF file.

items.question: Does my text get uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. Rendering happens in your browser on the page, so your text stays on your device during normal use.

items.question: Why does long text stop before the end?
items.answer: This release is a single-page handwriting converter. If your text exceeds the available writing area, the page shows how many extra lines would need a second page.

items.question: Which paper styles are available?
items.answer: You can switch between plain white paper, notebook paper, yellow legal pad, exam answer sheet paper, and vintage paper sheet backgrounds.

height_calculator.title: Frequently Asked Questions
height_calculator.description: Quick answers about height units, conversion math, and how this converter works.

items.question: How does this height converter work?
items.answer: You enter a height, choose the unit you have, choose the unit you want, and the tool converts that value using exact standard conversion factors.

items.question: Is this height conversion exact?
items.answer: Yes. This tool uses exact standard conversion factors such as 1 inch = 2.54 centimeters and 1 foot = 12 inches.

items.question: Which height units can I convert here?
items.answer: You can convert centimeters, meters, feet, inches, and feet plus inches. The result area also shows the same value in the other common formats.

items.question: Why are feet and inches split into two inputs?
items.answer: Feet and inches are commonly entered as two parts, such as 5 feet 9 inches. The main field takes feet and the extra field takes inches.

items.question: Do I only get one result?
items.answer: No. The main result shows the target unit you selected, and below it the tool also shows centimeters, meters, feet, inches, and feet plus inches for convenience.
